items.markdown:
**Sync data** (short for data synchronization) is `the process of ensuring that files, settings, or information remain consistent and up-to-date across multiple devices or systems` . If you add a contact on your phone, syncing automatically updates your tablet or laptop so you have the exact same information everywhere.[](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs) [[1]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs)[[2]](https://www.lenovo.com/ph/en/glossary/sync/)[[3]](https://resources.activepieces.com/glossary/data-sync)

How Data Syncing Works

Data syncing acts as an invisible bridge between your devices and cloud servers (like Google Drive or Microsoft OneDrive).[](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs) [[1]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs)[[2]](https://www.lenovo.com/gb/en/glossary/sync/)

- **Real-time or Batch:** Changes are either sent continuously in real-time or periodically in batches.
- **One-Way or Two-Way:** Changes can either be pushed in one direction (source to device) or merge mutually (changes on device A apply to device B and vice versa).[](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/data-synchronization/) [[1]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/data-synchronization/)

Common Examples

- **Cloud Storage:** Editing a document on your desktop and having it immediately available on your smartphone.
- **Messaging Apps:** Checking your chat history on your computer and picking up right where you left off on your phone.
- **Health & Fitness:** Your smartwatch steps and heart rate automatically populating in your phone's health dashboard.[](https://www.lenovo.com/gb/en/glossary/sync/) [[1]](https://www.lenovo.com/gb/en/glossary/sync/)[[2]](https://www.lenovo.com/ph/en/glossary/sync/)[[3]](https://library.mosse-institute.com/articles/2023/08/mobile-device-synchronization.html)[[4]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V8xetPBE_dk&t=49)[[5]](https://healthsync.app/about/)

Why It’s Important

- **Accessibility:** You can seamlessly transition your work or personal tasks from one device to another without manual transfers.
- **Backup & Recovery:** If a device is lost or damaged, the synced data remains secure in the cloud, allowing you to restore it instantly.
- **Efficiency:** Eliminates the need to repeatedly copy, paste, or re-enter data across different applications.[](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/data-synchronization/) [[1]](https://www.workato.com/the-connector/data-synchronization/)[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs)[[3]](https://www.lenovo.com/ph/en/glossary/sync/)[[4]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V8xetPBE_dk&t=49)[[5]](https://www.lenovo.com/us/en/glossary/sync/)[[6]](https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/data-synchronization)

*Note: Syncing requires an active internet connection and can consume mobile data, which is why devices often default to syncing only over Wi-Fi.* [](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs) [[1]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cceZj46hevs)[[2]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V8xetPBE_dk&t=49)

expanded_element.title: Synchronization - Wikipedia
expanded_element.description: Synchronization is the coordination of events to operate a system in unison. For example, the conductor of an orchestra keeps the orchestra synchronized or in time. Systems that operate with all parts in synchrony are said to be synchronous or in sync—and those that are not are asynchronous.
